revision.text: #redirect [[タキリビメ]]

'''多比理岐志麻流美神'''（たひりきしまるみのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
『[[古事記]]』の[[大国主神]]の系譜に5世の孫神として登場するが、神名のみで事跡も語られていないため、その名義も不明である。ちなみに、大国主神の4世の孫神、'''甕主日子神'''（みかぬしひこのかみ）が[[淤加美神]]の娘'''比那良志毘売'''（ひならしびめ）を娶って生んだ子神と記述されている。
<!-- == 祀る神社 == * [[多比鹿神社]]（[[三重県]][[三重郡]][[菰野町]]） --> == 参考文献 == * [[西郷信綱]]『古事記注釈』第3巻、[[ちくま学芸文庫]]、平成17年 ISBN 4-480-08913-6
{{DEFAULTSORT:たひりきしまるみのかみ}} [[Category:日本の神]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''多比理岐志麻流美神'''（たひりきしまるみのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
==概要・系譜== 『[[古事記]]』の[[大国主神]]の系譜に5世の孫神として登場するが、神名のみで事跡も語られていないため、その名義も不明である。ちなみに、大国主神の4世の孫神、'''[[甕主日子神]]'''（みかぬしひこのかみ）が[[淤加美神]]の娘'''[[比那良志毘売]]'''（ひならしびめ）を娶って生んだ子神と記述されている。
== 祀る神社 == * [[多比鹿神社]]（[[三重県]][[三重郡]][[菰野町]]）
== 参考文献 == * [[西郷信綱]]『古事記注釈』第3巻、[[ちくま学芸文庫]]、平成17年 ISBN 4-480-08913-6
{{DEFAULTSORT:たひりきしまるみのかみ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''多比理岐志麻流美神'''（たひりきしまるみのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
==概要== 『[[古事記]]』の[[大国主神]]の系譜に5世の孫神として登場するが、神名のみで事跡も語られていないため、その名義も不明である。 「タヒリキ」・「シマルミ」から平坦な島と海と捉えることもできるが真相は不明。
==系譜== 大国主神の4世の孫神、'''[[甕主日子神]]'''（みかぬしひこのかみ）が[[淤加美神]]の娘'''[[比那良志毘売]]'''（ひならしびめ）を娶って生んだ子神と記述されている。
== 祀る神社 == * [[多比鹿神社]]（[[三重県]][[三重郡]][[菰野町]]）
== 参考文献 == * [[西郷信綱]]『古事記注釈』第3巻、[[ちくま学芸文庫]]、平成17年 ISBN 4-480-08913-6
{{DEFAULTSORT:たひりきしまるみのかみ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''多比理岐志麻流美神'''（たひりきしまるみのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
==概要== 『[[古事記]]』の[[大国主神]]の系譜に5世の孫神として登場するが、神名のみで事跡も語られていないため、その名義も不明である。 「タヒリキ」・「シマルミ」から平坦な島と海と捉えることもできるが真相は不明。
==系譜== 大国主神の4世の孫神、'''[[甕主日子神]]'''（みかぬしひこのかみ）が[[淤加美神]]の娘'''[[比那良志毘売]]'''（ひならしびめ）を娶って生んだ神で、[[比々羅木之其花麻豆美神]]の娘'''[[活玉前玉比売神]]'''を娶って'''[[美呂浪神]]'''を産んだ。
== 祀る神社 == * [[多比鹿神社]]（[[三重県]][[三重郡]][[菰野町]]）

==概要== 『[[古事記]]』の[[大国主神]]の系譜に5世の孫神として登場するが、神名のみで事跡も語られていないため、その名義も不明である。十七世神（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱である[[国津神]]。 「タヒリキ」・「シマルミ」から平坦な島と海と捉えることもできるが真相は不明。
==系譜== 大国主神の4世の孫神、'''[[甕主日子神]]'''（みかぬしひこのかみ）が[[淤加美神]]の娘'''[[比那良志毘売]]'''（ひならしびめ）を娶って生んだ神で、[[比々羅木之其花麻豆美神]]の娘'''[[活玉前玉比売神]]'''を娶って'''[[美呂浪神]]'''を産んだ。

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 多比理岐志麻流美神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第十一代 | 先代= [[甕主日子神]] | 次代= [[美呂浪神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 多比理岐志麻流美神 | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = [[甕主日子神]] | 母 = [[比那良志毘売]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[活玉前玉比売神]] | 子 = [[美呂浪神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[多比鹿神社]]等 }}
'''多比理岐志麻流美神'''（たひりきしまるみのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
==概要== 『[[古事記]]』の[[大国主神]]の系譜に5世の孫神として登場するが、神名のみで事跡も語られていないため、その名義も不明である。[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱である[[国津神]]。 「タヒリキ」・「シマルミ」から平坦な島と海と捉えることもできるが真相は不明。
==系譜== [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] [[大国主神]]の4世孫の[[甕主日子神]]が[[淤加美神]]の娘[[比那良志毘売]]を娶って生んだ神で、[[比々羅木之其花麻豆美神]]の娘[[活玉前玉比売神]]を娶って[[美呂浪神]]を産んだ。
== 祀る神社 == * [[多比鹿神社]]（[[三重県]][[三重郡]][[菰野町]]） * [[当島八幡神社]]（[[広島県]][[福山市]][[駅家町]]）
== 参考文献 == * [[西郷信綱]]『古事記注釈』第3巻、[[ちくま学芸文庫]]、平成17年 ISBN 4-480-08913-6
{{DEFAULTSORT:たひりきしまるみのかみ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] {{shinto-stub}}

This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are..."
revision.text:
{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.
== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:17 spirits of the world|ja|Category:十七世神|fr|Category:17 esprits du monde}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] }}

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.
== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:17 spirits of the world|ja|Category:十七世神|fr|Category:17 esprits du monde}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] }}

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 多比理岐志麻流美神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第十一代 | 先代 = [[ミカヌシヒコ|甕主日子神]] | 次代 = [[ミロナミ|美呂浪神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 多比理岐志麻流美神（タヒリキシマルミノカミ） | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = [[ミカヌシヒコ|甕主日子神]] | 母 = [[ヒナラシビメ|比那良志毘売]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[イクタマサキタマヒメ|活玉前玉比売神]] | 子 = 美呂浪神 | 宮 = | 神社 = 多比鹿神社 | 記紀等 = 古事記 }} '''多比理岐志麻流美神'''は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』の[[大国主神]]の系譜に5世の孫神として登場するが、神名のみで事跡も語られていないため、その名義も不明である。[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱である[[国津神]]。「タヒリキ」・「シマルミ」から平坦な島と海と捉えることもできるが真相は不明。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] [[大国主神]]の4世孫の甕主日子神が[[淤加美神]]の娘の比那良志毘売を娶って生んだ神で、[[ヒヒラギノソノハナマヅミ|比々羅木之其花麻豆美神]]の娘の活玉前玉比売神を娶って美呂浪神を産んだ。 == 祀る神社 == * 多比鹿神社（[[三重県]][[三重郡]][[菰野町]]田光） - 主祭神 == 参考文献 == * [[西郷信綱]]『古事記注釈』第3巻、[[ちくま学芸文庫]]、平成17年 ISBN 4-480-08913-6 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たひりきしまるみのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

{{改名提案|タヒリキシマルミ|t=ノート:十七世神|date=2024年8月}} {{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 多比理岐志麻流美神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第十一代 | 先代 = [[ミカヌシヒコ|甕主日子神]] | 次代 = [[ミロナミ|美呂浪神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 多比理岐志麻流美神（タヒリキシマルミノカミ） | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = 甕主日子神 | 母 = [[ヒナラシビメ|比那良志毘売]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[イクタマサキタマヒメ|活玉前玉比売神]] | 子 = 美呂浪神 | 宮 = | 神社 = 多比鹿神社 | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''多比理岐志麻流美神'''は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』の[[大国主神]]の系譜に5世の孫神として登場するが、神名のみで事跡も語られていないため、その名義も不明である。[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱である[[国津神]]。「タヒリキ」・「シマルミ」から平坦な島と海と捉えることもできるが真相は不明。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] [[大国主神]]の4世孫の甕主日子神が[[淤加美神]]の娘の比那良志毘売を娶って生んだ神で、[[ヒヒラギノソノハナマヅミ|比々羅木之其花麻豆美神]]の娘の活玉前玉比売神を娶って美呂浪神を産んだ。 == 祀る神社 == * 多比鹿神社（[[三重県]][[三重郡]][[菰野町]]田光） - 主祭神 == 参考文献 == * <!-- さいごう -->{{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記注釈 |date=2005年8月 |publisher=[[筑摩書房]] |author=[[西郷信綱]] |series=[[ちくま学芸文庫]] |isbn=4-480-08913-6 |volume=第3巻}} == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/tahirikishimaruminokami/ 多比理岐志麻流美神 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%a4%9a%e6%af%94%e9%b9%bf%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 三重県神社庁教化委員会 » 多比鹿神社] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たひりきしまるみのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

revision.text: {{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 多比理岐志麻流美神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第十一代 | 先代 = [[甕主日子神]] | 次代 = [[美呂浪神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 多比理岐志麻流美神（タヒリキシマルミノカミ） | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = 甕主日子神 | 母 = [[比那良志毘売]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[活玉前玉比売神]] | 子 = 美呂浪神 | 宮 = | 神社 = 多比鹿神社 | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''多比理岐志麻流美神'''（タヒリキシマルミ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』の[[大国主神]]の系譜に5世の孫神として登場するが、神名のみで事跡も語られていないため、その名義も不明である。[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱である[[国津神]]。「タヒリキ」・「シマルミ」から平坦な島と海と捉えることもできるが真相は不明。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] [[大国主神]]の4世孫の甕主日子神が[[淤加美神]]の娘の比那良志毘売を娶って生んだ神で、[[ヒヒラギノソノハナマヅミ|比々羅木之其花麻豆美神]]の娘の活玉前玉比売神を娶って美呂浪神を産んだ。 == 祀る神社 == * 多比鹿神社（[[三重県]][[三重郡]][[菰野町]]田光） - 主祭神 == 参考文献 == * <!-- さいごう -->{{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記注釈 |date=2005年8月 |publisher=[[筑摩書房]] |author=[[西郷信綱]] |series=[[ちくま学芸文庫]] |isbn=4-480-08913-6 |volume=第3巻}} == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/tahirikishimaruminokami/ 多比理岐志麻流美神 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%a4%9a%e6%af%94%e9%b9%bf%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 三重県神社庁教化委員会 » 多比鹿神社] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たひりきしまるみのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

revision.comment: 2 revisions imported: importing my simple english wikipedia drafts for the 17 spirits of the world
revision.text:
{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.
== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:17 spirits of the world|ja|Category:十七世神|fr|Category:17 esprits du monde}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] }}

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.
== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories|[[Category:Recently imported simple english 17 spirits of the world]] [[Category:original pages]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami]]{{ill|Category:17 spirits of the world|ja|Category:十七世神|fr|Category:17 esprits du monde}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] }}

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.
== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|goodbye $1/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|goodbye $1/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother = |ignore = is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|goodbye $1/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|goodbye $1/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.goodbye $1 | Name = Tahirikishimarumi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}} 11th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Mikamunohikono Kami|ja|甕主日子神}} | Successor = {{ill|Mironami no Kami|ja|美呂浪神}} | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 多比理岐志麻流美神 (Tahirikishimarumi no Kami) | Alternate Name= | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}} | mother = {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} | Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} | children = {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Tahika Shrine|ja|多比鹿神社}} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Tahirikishimarumi no Kami''' (多比理岐志麻流美神, タヒリキシマルミ) is a deity mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
{{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}} was his father. This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.

== Overview == Appearing in the genealogical record of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is described as a fifth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi. However, the deity's name is mentioned without any associated myths or deeds, leaving its significance and etymology ambiguous. Based on the components "Tahiriki" and "Shimarumi," the name may imply a flat island and sea, but its exact meaning is uncertain.

'''Tahirikishimarumi no Kami''' (多比理岐志麻流美神, タヒリキシマルミ) is a deity mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
{{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}} was his father. This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Overview == Appearing in the genealogical record of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is described as a fifth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi. However, the deity's name is mentioned without any associated myths or deeds, leaving its significance and etymology ambiguous. Based on the components "Tahiriki" and "Shimarumi," the name may imply a flat island and sea, but its exact meaning is uncertain.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is the child of {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}, and {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}. Tahirikishimarumi no Kami marries {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}}, the daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}, and has a child, {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Shrine == * '''Tahika Shrine''' ({{ill|Komono|ja|菰野町}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) - The main shrine dedicated to Tahirikishimarumi no Kami.

revision.text:
{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|goodbye $1/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|goodbye $1/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother = |ignore = is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|goodbye $1/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|goodbye $1/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.goodbye $1 | Name = Tahirikishimarumi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}} 11th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Mikamunohikono Kami|ja|甕主日子神}} | Successor = {{ill|Mironami no Kami|ja|美呂浪神}} | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 多比理岐志麻流美神 (Tahirikishimarumi no Kami) | Alternate Name= | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}} | mother = {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} | Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} | children = {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Tahika Shrine|ja|多比鹿神社}} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Tahirikishimarumi no Kami''' (多比理岐志麻流美神, タヒリキシマルミ) is a deity mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
{{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}} was his father. This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Overview == Appearing in the genealogical record of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is described as a fifth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi. However, the deity's name is mentioned without any associated myths or deeds, leaving its significance and etymology ambiguous. Based on the components "Tahiriki" and "Shimarumi," the name may imply a flat island and sea, but its exact meaning is uncertain.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is the child of {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}, and {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}. Tahirikishimarumi no Kami marries {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}}, the daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}, and has a child, {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Shrine == * '''Tahika Shrine''' ({{ill|Komono|ja|菰野町}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) - The main shrine dedicated to Tahirikishimarumi no Kami.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=[[Nobutsuna Saigō]]|title=Annotated Kojiki (古事記注釈)|date=August 2005|publisher=[[Chikuma Shobō]]|series=[[Chikuma Gakugei Bunko]]|volume=Vol. 3|isbn=4-480-08913-6}}

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother = |ignore = is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.goodbye $1 | Name = Tahirikishimarumi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}} 11th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Mikamunohikono Kami|ja|甕主日子神}} | Successor = {{ill|Mironami no Kami|ja|美呂浪神}} | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 多比理岐志麻流美神 (Tahirikishimarumi no Kami) | Alternate Name= | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}} | mother = {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} | Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} | children = {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Tahika Shrine|ja|多比鹿神社}} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Tahirikishimarumi no Kami''' (多比理岐志麻流美神, タヒリキシマルミ) is a deity mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
{{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}} was his father. This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Overview == Appearing in the genealogical record of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is described as a fifth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi. However, the deity's name is mentioned without any associated myths or deeds, leaving its significance and etymology ambiguous. Based on the components "Tahiriki" and "Shimarumi," the name may imply a flat island and sea, but its exact meaning is uncertain.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is the child of {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}, and {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}. Tahirikishimarumi no Kami marries {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}}, the daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}, and has a child, {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Shrine == * '''Tahika Shrine''' ({{ill|Komono|ja|菰野町}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) - The main shrine dedicated to Tahirikishimarumi no Kami.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother = |ignore = is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.goodbye $1 | Name = Tahirikishimarumi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}} 11th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Mikamunohikono Kami|ja|甕主日子神}} | Successor = {{ill|Mironami no Kami|ja|美呂浪神}} | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 多比理岐志麻流美神 (Tahirikishimarumi no Kami) | Alternate Name= | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}} | mother = {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} | Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} | children = {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Tahika Shrine|ja|多比鹿神社}} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Tahirikishimarumi no Kami''' (多比理岐志麻流美神, タヒリキシマルミ) is a deity mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
{{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}} was his father. This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Overview == Appearing in the genealogical record of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is described as a fifth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi. However, the deity's name is mentioned without any associated myths or deeds, leaving its significance and etymology ambiguous. Based on the components "Tahiriki" and "Shimarumi," the name may imply a flat island and sea, but its exact meaning is uncertain.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is the child of {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}, and {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}. Tahirikishimarumi no Kami marries {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}}, the daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}, and has a child, {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Shrine == * '''Tahika Shrine''' ({{ill|Komono|ja|菰野町}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) - The main shrine dedicated to Tahirikishimarumi no Kami.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=[[Nobutsuna Saigō]]|title=Annotated Kojiki (古事記注釈)|date=August 2005|publisher=[[Chikuma Shobō]]|series=[[Chikuma Gakugei Bunko]]|volume=Vol. 3|isbn=4-480-08913-6}}
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/tahirikishimaruminokami/ Tahirikishimarumi no Kami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%a4%9a%e6%af%94%e9%b9%bf%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Mie Prefecture Shrine Association – Tahika Shrine]

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother = |ignore = is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.goodbye $1 | Name = Tahirikishimarumi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}} 11th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Mikamunohikono Kami|ja|甕主日子神}} | Successor = {{ill|Mironami no Kami|ja|美呂浪神}} | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 多比理岐志麻流美神 (Tahirikishimarumi no Kami) | Alternate Name= | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}} | mother = {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} | Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} | children = {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Tahika Shrine|ja|多比鹿神社}} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Tahirikishimarumi no Kami''' (多比理岐志麻流美神, タヒリキシマルミ) is a deity mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
{{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}} was his father. This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Overview == Appearing in the genealogical record of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is described as a fifth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi. However, the deity's name is mentioned without any associated myths or deeds, leaving its significance and etymology ambiguous. Based on the components "Tahiriki" and "Shimarumi," the name may imply a flat island and sea, but its exact meaning is uncertain.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is the child of {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}, and {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}. Tahirikishimarumi no Kami marries {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}}, the daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}, and has a child, {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Shrine == * '''Tahika Shrine''' ({{ill|Komono|ja|菰野町}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) - The main shrine dedicated to Tahirikishimarumi no Kami.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=[[Nobutsuna Saigō]]|title=Annotated Kojiki (古事記注釈)|date=August 2005|publisher=[[Chikuma Shobō]]|series=[[Chikuma Gakugei Bunko]]|volume=Vol. 3|isbn=4-480-08913-6}}

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother = |ignore = is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.goodbye $1 | Name = Tahirikishimarumi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}} 11th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Mikamunohikono Kami|ja|甕主日子神}} | Successor = {{ill|Mironami no Kami|ja|美呂浪神}} | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 多比理岐志麻流美神 (Tahirikishimarumi no Kami) | Alternate Name= | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}} | mother = {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} | Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} | children = {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Tahika Shrine|ja|多比鹿神社}} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Tahirikishimarumi no Kami''' (多比理岐志麻流美神, タヒリキシマルミ) is a deity mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
{{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}} was his father. This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Overview == Appearing in the genealogical record of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is described as a fifth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi. However, the deity's name is mentioned without any associated myths or deeds, leaving its significance and etymology ambiguous. Based on the components "Tahiriki" and "Shimarumi," the name may imply a flat island and sea, but its exact meaning is uncertain.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is the child of {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}, and {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}. Tahirikishimarumi no Kami marries {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}}, the daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}, and has a child, {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Shrine == * '''Tahika Shrine''' ({{ill|Komono|ja|菰野町}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) ** The main shrine dedicated to Tahirikishimarumi no Kami.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=[[Nobutsuna Saigō]]|title=Annotated Kojiki (古事記注釈)|date=August 2005|publisher=[[Chikuma Shobō]]|series=[[Chikuma Gakugei Bunko]]|volume=Vol. 3|isbn=4-480-08913-6}}
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/tahirikishimaruminokami/ Tahirikishimarumi no Kami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%a4%9a%e6%af%94%e9%b9%bf%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Mie Prefecture Shrine Association – Tahika Shrine]

revision.text:
{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother = |ignore = is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.goodbye $1 | Name = Tahirikishimarumi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}} 11th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Mikamunohikono Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami}} | Successor = {{ill|Mironami no Kami|ja|美呂浪神}} | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 多比理岐志麻流美神 (Tahirikishimarumi no Kami) | Alternate Name= | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami}} | mother = {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} | Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} | children = {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Tahika Shrine|ja|多比鹿神社}} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Tahirikishimarumi no Kami''' (多比理岐志麻流美神, タヒリキシマルミ) is a deity mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
{{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami}} was his father. This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Overview == Appearing in the genealogical record of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is described as a fifth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi. However, the deity's name is mentioned without any associated myths or deeds, leaving its significance and etymology ambiguous. Based on the components "Tahiriki" and "Shimarumi," the name may imply a flat island and sea, but its exact meaning is uncertain.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is the child of {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}, and {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}. Tahirikishimarumi no Kami marries {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}}, the daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}, and has a child, {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Shrine == * '''Tahika Shrine''' ({{ill|Komono|ja|菰野町}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) ** The main shrine dedicated to Tahirikishimarumi no Kami.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=[[Nobutsuna Saigō]]|title=Annotated Kojiki (古事記注釈)|date=August 2005|publisher=[[Chikuma Shobō]]|series=[[Chikuma Gakugei Bunko]]|volume=Vol. 3|isbn=4-480-08913-6}}
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/tahirikishimaruminokami/ Tahirikishimarumi no Kami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%a4%9a%e6%af%94%e9%b9%bf%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Mie Prefecture Shrine Association – Tahika Shrine]

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother = |ignore = is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.goodbye $1 | Name = Tahirikishimarumi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}} 11th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Mikamunohikono Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami}} | Successor = {{ill|Mironami no Kami|ja|美呂浪神}} | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 多比理岐志麻流美神 (Tahirikishimarumi no Kami) | Alternate Name= | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami}} | mother = {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} | Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} | children = {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Tahika Shrine|ja|多比鹿神社}} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Tahirikishimarumi no Kami''' (多比理岐志麻流美神, タヒリキシマルミ) is a deity mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
{{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami}} was his father. This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Overview == Appearing in the genealogical record of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is described as a fifth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi. However, the deity's name is mentioned without any associated myths or deeds, leaving its significance and etymology ambiguous. Based on the components "Tahiriki" and "Shimarumi," the name may imply a flat island and sea, but its exact meaning is uncertain.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is the child of {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}, and {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}. Tahirikishimarumi no Kami marries {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}}, the daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}, and has a child, {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Shrine == * '''Tahika Shrine''' ({{ill|Komono|ja|菰野町}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) ** The main shrine dedicated to Tahirikishimarumi no Kami.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=[[Nobutsuna Saigō]]|title=Annotated Kojiki (古事記注釈)|date=August 2005|publisher=[[Chikuma Shobō]]|series=[[Chikuma Gakugei Bunko]]|volume=Vol. 3|isbn=4-480-08913-6}}
== External Links == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/tahirikishimaruminokami/ Tahirikishimarumi no Kami – Kokugakuin University Classical Culture Project] * [http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%a4%9a%e6%af%94%e9%b9%bf%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Mie Prefecture Shrine Association – Tahika Shrine]

Tahirikishimarumi no Kami marries {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}}, the daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}, and has a child, {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother = |ignore = is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|user:immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.goodbye $1 | Name = Tahirikishimarumi no Kami | Generation = {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神}} 11th generation | Predecessor = {{ill|Mikamunohikono Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami}} | Successor = {{ill|Mironami no Kami|ja|美呂浪神}} | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | Full Name = 多比理岐志麻流美神 (Tahirikishimarumi no Kami) | Alternate Name= | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = | Mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami}} | mother = {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} | Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} | children = {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Tahika Shrine|ja|多比鹿神社}} | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} }}
'''Tahirikishimarumi no Kami''' (多比理岐志麻流美神, タヒリキシマルミ) is a deity mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
{{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami}} was his father. This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Overview == Appearing in the genealogical record of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}} in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is described as a fifth-generation descendant of Ōkuninushi. However, the deity's name is mentioned without any associated myths or deeds, leaving its significance and etymology ambiguous. Based on the components "Tahiriki" and "Shimarumi," the name may imply a flat island and sea, but its exact meaning is uncertain.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (from the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]] Tahirikishimarumi no Kami is the child of {{ill|Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|ja|甕主日子神|1=Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi-no-Hiko-no-Kami}}, the fourth-generation descendant of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神}}, and {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}. Tahirikishimarumi no Kami marries {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}}, the daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|1=Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}, and has a child, {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
== Shrine == * '''Tahika Shrine''' ({{ill|Komono|ja|菰野町}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}) ** The main shrine dedicated to Tahirikishimarumi no Kami.
== References == * {{Cite book|author=[[Nobutsuna Saigō]]|title=Annotated Kojiki (古事記注釈)|date=August 2005|publisher=[[Chikuma Shobō]]|series=[[Chikuma Gakugei Bunko]]|volume=Vol. 3|isbn=4-480-08913-6}}
